accordion.description: A disclosure component that groups collapsible sections to let users reveal or hide related content.

alert-dialog.description: A modal overlay component that requires an explicit user action or confirmation before closing.

app-layout.description: A structural component that wraps the entire application to define its global layout shell.

avatar.description: A data display component that renders a user or entity image with an optional fallback visual.

badge.description: A data display component that presents a compact label, count, or status indicator.

bg-image.description: A data display component that applies an absolutely positioned background image to a container.

box.description: A generic layout primitive that serves as a low-level container for grouping and styling content.

blockquote.description: A typography component that highlights a quoted passage of text with stylized indentation or borders.

border-beam.description: An effect component that renders an animated, traveling beam of light around an element's border.

breadcrumbs.description: A navigation component that displays a linear path of links showing the user's current location in a hierarchy.

button.description: An interactive component that triggers an action, event, or operation when clicked or tapped.

callout.description: A feedback component that highlights important messages or notices within a visually distinct container.

card.description: A data display component that groups related content, imagery, and actions onto a single contained surface.

center.description: An alignment component that centers its child elements both horizontally and vertically.

checkbox.description: A form component that allows users to select one or more independent options from a list.

checkbox-card.description: A form component that pairs a standard checkbox state with a selectable structural card layout.

close-button.description: An interactive component specifically styled to dismiss or close parent overlays, alerts, or panels.

code.description: A typography component that displays inline or block text using a monospaced font for code snippets.

collapsible.description: A disclosure component that toggles the visibility of an isolated content region.

color-swatch.description: A data display component that presents a solid color block for color previewing or selection.

container.description: A structural component that constrains content width and applies responsive margins to maintain layout consistency.

context-menu.description: An overlay component that reveals a targeted list of actions when triggered by a right-click or long-press.

counter.description: An effect component that displays numerical values with animated transitions during value changes.

description.description: A typography component designed for secondary or descriptive prose with standard spacing and hierarchy.

dialog.description: A modal overlay component that interrupts the user flow to capture focus for vital content or interactions.

display.description: A typography component that renders extra-large text for high-impact visual statements and headlines.

design-grid.description: A utility layout component that overlays a visual grid system to assist with layout alignment and development.

drawer.description: An overlay component that slides out from the edge of the screen to present secondary content.

dropdown-menu.description: An overlay component that displays a contextual menu of choices when triggered by a button.

empty-state.description: A feedback component that provides guidance, context, or action items when a view has no data to display.

field.description: A form component that encapsulates a form input alongside its associated label, description, and validation errors.

flex.description: A layout primitive based on CSS Flexbox for distributing space and aligning item rows or columns.

float.description: An alignment component that floats elements within text-based flows to allow inline word-wrapping.

grid.description: A layout primitive based on CSS Grid for arranging items into strict, structured columns and rows.

group.description: A layout primitive that clusters related items horizontally or vertically with unified borders and spacing.

headline.description: A typography component used for prominent structural headings across content sections.

highlight.description: A typography component that emphasizes specific words using background or pseudo-element visual markers.

hover-card.description: An overlay component that reveals rich preview content when a pointer hovers over a target element.

hstack.description: A layout primitive that arranges child elements in a strict horizontal row with uniform spacing.

icon.description: A data display component that renders a scalable vector graphic to visually communicate meaning.

icon-box.description: A data display component that nests an icon within a styled, geometrically bounded container.

icon-button.description: An interactive component that packages an icon inside a click target for compact layouts.

image.description: A data display component that renders responsive images with baked-in aspect ratio and fallback controls.

input.description: A form component that accepts single-line textual input from the user.

in-view.description: An effect component that triggers entrance transitions or animations when it passes into the viewport.

kbd.description: A typography component that formats keyboard inputs, shortcuts, or hotkeys.

lead.description: A typography component that emphasizes introduction paragraphs with expanded text styling.

link.description: A navigation component that renders an interactive hyperlink for browser navigation.

list.description: A typography component that organizes textual points or child items into ordered or unordered blocks.

loader.description: A feedback component that displays an animation indicating an ongoing system background process.

logo.description: A data display component optimized for rendering brand assets and identities cleanly.

mark.description: A typography component that applies a subtle inline background color highlight to emphasize key terms.

marquee.description: A data display component that horizontally loops a continuous, infinitely scrolling banner of content.

menubar.description: A navigation component that implements a persistent horizontal bar of top-level application menus.

native-select.description: A form component that implements the browser's unstyled drop-down interface for menu choices.

nav-button.description: A navigation component styled as a button used specifically for route changes and link navigation.

nav-link.description: A navigation component that applies semantic active-states to inline navigational hyperlinks.

navigation-menu.description: A navigation component that presents structural site hierarchies via drop-down panels or links.

overlay.description: An overlay component that darkens or blurs the background behind active modal content.

password.description: A form component that masks text strings for secure password entry, featuring optional visibility toggles.

pin-input.description: A form component split into individual character blocks for entering authentication codes or pins.

popover.description: An overlay component that floats complex descriptive content or micro-forms relative to a reference trigger.

progress.description: A data display component that maps numerical completeness percentages across a tracking bar.

radio.description: A form component that restricts users to selecting exactly one option from an associated button set.

radio-card.description: A form component that adapts exclusive radio selection mechanisms into larger, data-rich card layouts.

scroll-area.description: A layout component that replaces browser defaults with sleek, custom-designed viewport scrollbars.

section.description: A structural component that groups layouts into isolated block regions separated by consistent padding rules.

select.description: A form component that provides a styled custom selection menu for picking singular values.

separator.description: A layout component that creates thin horizontal or vertical rule lines to divide layout zones.

skeleton.description: A feedback component that uses flashing abstract blocks to mask incomplete content during network requests.

slider.description: A form component that sets values by scrubbing a dial slider handle along a continuous range track.

stack.description: A layout primitive that establishes vertical rhythm by adding identical margins between nested elements.

status.description: A feedback component that renders a color-coded node representing systemic status states.

switch.description: A form component modeled after a hardware toggle switch to quickly commit binary settings.

table.description: A data display component designed to structure structured, multi-column datasets inside rows and cells.

tabs.description: A navigation component that switches structural content views without reloading by clicking button panes.

text.description: A typography component acting as a primitive wrapper for inline text.

textarea.description: A form component providing adjustable multi-line bounding inputs for lengthy textual responses.

text-slide.description: An effect component that applies sliding animations to alphanumeric strings when mounted.

title.description: A typography component used to style distinct page titles or modal headings with proper weights.

toast.description: A feedback component displaying brief, self-destructing notification prompts at the corners of viewports.

toggle.description: A form component implementing basic binary choice selections on individual custom button frames.

toggle-group.description: A form component tracking multi-select button arrays that retain clicked or unclicked selections.

tooltip.description: An overlay component displaying brief hints or string text descriptions over focused interactive items.

vstack.description: A layout primitive that strictly chains its layout elements into automated vertical tracks with distinct gap metrics.
